---
title: "gender_slur_s2_data"
output: html_document
date: "2025-03-20"
---

```{r}
library(lmerTest)
library(ggplot2)
```



```{r}
data <- read.csv("/Users/kalle/Desktop/gender_slur_paper/gender_slur_s2_data.csv")
```

```{r}


library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

data <- data %>%
  mutate(displayed_sentence = str_replace(displayed_sentence, "<q>The ", "<q>That "))
```


```{r}
# Count TEST participants before removal
test_count <- sum(data$submission_id == "TEST")
cat("Number of TEST participants to be removed:", test_count, "\n")

# Remove participants with submission_id value "TEST"
data <- data[data$submission_id != "TEST", ]

# Verify removal
cat("Number of participants remaining:", length(unique(data$submission_id)), "\n")
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Create a new data frame that has only one row per submission_id
data_unique <- data %>%
  distinct(submission_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

# Calculate mean age and SD from the unique subset
mean_age <- mean(data_unique$age, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_age   <- sd(data_unique$age, na.rm = TRUE)

mean_age
sd_age

```

```{r}
# Filter for Control1 and Control2 items
control_data <- data[data$itemname %in% c("Control1", "Control2"), ]

# Identify participants with Individual responses in control items
problem_participants <- unique(control_data$submission_id[control_data$response == "Individual"])

# Count these participants
num_problem_participants <- length(problem_participants)
cat("Number of participants with Individual responses in control items:", num_problem_participants, "\n")

# Remove these participants from the dataset
if (num_problem_participants > 0) {
  data <- data[!data$submission_id %in% problem_participants, ]
  cat("These participants have been removed from the dataset\n")
  cat("Number of participants remaining:", length(unique(data$submission_id)), "\n")
}
```

```{r}
# Check for participants who responded "Group" for filler items 9-14
filler_data <- data[data$itemname %in% paste0("filler", 9:14), ]

# Identify participants with Group responses in these filler items
filler_problem_participants <- unique(filler_data$submission_id[filler_data$response == "Group"])

# Count these participants
num_filler_problem_participants <- length(filler_problem_participants)
cat("Number of participants with Group responses in filler items 9-14:", num_filler_problem_participants, "\n")

# Display participant IDs if there are any
if (num_filler_problem_participants > 1) {
  cat("Problematic participants:\n")
  print(filler_problem_participants)
  
  # Remove these participants from the dataset
  data <- data[!data$submission_id %in% filler_problem_participants, ]
  cat("These participants have been removed from the dataset\n")
  cat("Number of participants remaining:", length(unique(data$submission_id)), "\n")
}
```

```{r}
# Step 0: Filter to keep only items that start with "item"
data_filtered <- data[grepl("^Item", data$itemname), ]

# Check how many rows were filtered out
cat("Original dataframe rows:", nrow(data), "\n")
cat("Filtered dataframe rows:", nrow(data_filtered), "\n")
cat("Rows removed:", nrow(data) - nrow(data_filtered), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Recalculate summary statistics focusing on group responses
summary_stats <- data_filtered %>%
  # Ensure pejorative_type and action are character vectors
  mutate(
    pejorative_type = as.character(pejorative_type),
    action = as.character(action)
  ) %>%
  # Group and summarize
  group_by(pejorative_type, action) %>%
  summarize(
    n = sum(!is.na(response)),
    individual_count = sum(response == "Individual", na.rm = TRUE),
    group_count = sum(response == "Group", na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_group = group_count / n,  # Calculate proportion of Group responses
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Calculate confidence intervals for group proportion
  mutate(
    se = sqrt((prop_group * (1 - prop_group)) / n),
    ci_lower = prop_group - 1.96 * se,
    ci_upper = prop_group + 1.96 * se
  )
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Example data transformation: convert prop_group to percentages 
# and CI bounds to percentage points
plot_data <- summary_stats %>%
  mutate(
    prop_group_pct = 100 * prop_group,
    ci_lower_pct = 100 * ci_lower,
    ci_upper_pct = 100 * ci_upper
  )

ggplot(plot_data, aes(
    x = pejorative_type,
    y = prop_group_pct,
    color = action,
    group = action
)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = ci_lower_pct, ymax = ci_upper_pct),
    width = 0.1,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.03)
  ) +
  geom_line(size = 0.8, position = position_dodge(width = 0.03)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, position = position_dodge(width = 0.03)) +
  labs(
    x = "Pejorative",
    y = "Group Directed Responses",
    color = "Action"
    # No title here
  ) +
  # Scale the y-axis as percentage, here using breaks at 20% increments
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 100),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20),
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%")
  ) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") + 
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray95"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 11),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5)
  )

```

```{r}
# Now convert to factors
data_filtered$pejorative_type <- as.factor(data_filtered$pejorative_type)
data_filtered$action <- as.factor(data_filtered$action)
data_filtered$response <- as.factor(data_filtered$response)
data_filtered$submission_id <- as.factor(data_filtered$submission_id)
data_filtered$itemname <- as.factor(data_filtered$itemname)

# Build the GLMER model with the properly transformed data
# Option 1: Add random slopes for action by participant
glmer_model <- glmer(
  response ~ pejorative_type * action  + 
    (1 + pejorative_type + action | submission_id) + 
    (1 | itemname),
  data = data_filtered,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
)


# View model summary
summary(glmer_model)
```




```{r}
# Create a summary dataframe for participant behavior (by pejorative_type only)
participant_summary <- data_filtered %>%
  group_by(submission_id, pejorative_type) %>%
  summarize(
    total_responses = n(),
    group_responses = sum(response == "Group", na.rm = TRUE),
    group_response_rate = (group_responses / total_responses) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
# Create the visualization
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(participant_summary, aes(x = pejorative_type, y = group_response_rate)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.5, color = "blue") +
  labs(
    x = "Pejorative Type",
    y = "Group-Directed Responses by Participant"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = function(x) paste0(x, "%"),
    breaks = seq(0, 100, by = 20)
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Calculate proportion of "Group" responses for each pejorative word by type
group_proportions <- data_filtered %>%
  group_by(pejorative_type, pejorative_word) %>%
  summarise(
    total_responses = n(),
    group_responses = sum(response == "Group", na.rm = TRUE),
    individual_responses = sum(response == "Individual", na.rm = TRUE),
    group_proportion = mean(response == "Group", na.rm = TRUE),
    group_percentage = group_proportion * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(pejorative_type, desc(group_percentage))

# Display the results
print("Proportion of Group responses by pejorative word and type:")
print(group_proportions)

# Summary by pejorative type
type_summary <- group_proportions %>%
  group_by(pejorative_type) %>%
  summarise(
    n_words = n(),
    mean_group_pct = mean(group_percentage),
    min_group_pct = min(group_percentage),
    max_group_pct = max(group_percentage),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print("Summary by pejorative type:")
print(type_summary)
```


```{reval=FALSE}
# DEBUG: Why are the frequentist and Bayesian estimates so different?

print("=== DEBUGGING MODEL DISCREPANCY ===")

# Check factor levels and coding
print("1. RESPONSE VARIABLE CODING:")
print("Original response levels:")
print(levels(data_filtered$response))
print("Original response table:")
print(table(data_filtered$response))

print("\nBayesian response_numeric coding:")
print(table(data_filtered$response_numeric))
print("Bayesian coding: Group=1, Individual=0")

# Check which response the GLMM is modeling
print("\n2. GLMM IS MODELING:")
glmm_contrast <- contrasts(data_filtered$response)
print("GLMM contrasts:")
print(glmm_contrast)

# The issue is likely here - let's check what the GLMM considers as "success"
print("GLMM is modeling probability of:")
if(is.factor(data_filtered$response)) {
  print(paste("Level:", levels(data_filtered$response)[2], "(coded as 1)"))
  print(paste("vs Level:", levels(data_filtered$response)[1], "(coded as 0)"))
}

print("\n3. PEJORATIVE TYPE CODING:")
print("Levels:")
print(levels(data_filtered$pejorative_type))
print("Contrasts:")
print(contrasts(data_filtered$pejorative_type))

print("\n4. ACTION CODING:")
print("Levels:")
print(levels(data_filtered$action))
print("Contrasts:")
print(contrasts(data_filtered$action))

# Let's refit the Bayesian model to match the GLMM exactly
print("\n5. FITTING BAYESIAN MODEL TO MATCH GLMM:")

# Use the same response variable as the GLMM
bayesian_model_corrected <- brm(
  response ~ pejorative_type * action + 
    (1 + pejorative_type + action | submission_id) + 
    (1 | itemname),
  data = data_filtered,
  family = bernoulli(),
  chains = 2,  # Fewer chains for faster debugging
  iter = 2000,
  warmup = 1000,
  cores = 2,
  seed = 123,
  control = list(adapt_delta = 0.95)
)

print("Corrected Bayesian model summary:")
print(fixef(bayesian_model_corrected))

# Compare the fixed effects
print("\n6. COMPARISON OF FIXED EFFECTS:")
glmm_summary <- summary(glmer_model)
glmm_coefs <- glmm_summary$coefficients[, "Estimate"]

bayesian_coefs <- fixef(bayesian_model_corrected)[, "Estimate"]

comparison <- data.frame(
  Parameter = names(glmm_coefs),
  GLMM = round(glmm_coefs, 3),
  Bayesian = round(bayesian_coefs, 3),
  Difference = round(bayesian_coefs - glmm_coefs, 3)
)

print(comparison)

# If they're still different, check the data being used
print("\n7. DATA CHECK:")
print(paste("GLMM used", nrow(glmer_model@frame), "observations"))
print(paste("Bayesian used", nrow(data_filtered), "observations"))

if(nrow(glmer_model@frame) != nrow(data_filtered)) {
  print("WARNING: Different number of observations used!")
}

# Now do the equivalence analysis with the corrected model
print("\n" %>% rep(3) %>% paste(collapse = ""))
print("CORRECTED EQUIVALENCE ANALYSIS")
print("=" %>% rep(40) %>% paste(collapse = ""))

# Extract posterior samples from corrected model
posterior_corrected <- as_draws_df(bayesian_model_corrected)
pejorative_samples_corrected <- posterior_corrected$b_pejorative_typeGOI

# ROPE analysis
rope_bounds <- c(-0.1, 0.1)
median_effect <- median(pejorative_samples_corrected)
ci_lower <- quantile(pejorative_samples_corrected, 0.025)
ci_upper <- quantile(pejorative_samples_corrected, 0.975)

in_rope <- sum(pejorative_samples_corrected >= rope_bounds[1] & 
               pejorative_samples_corrected <= rope_bounds[2])
pct_in_rope <- in_rope / length(pejorative_samples_corrected) * 100

print(paste("Median effect:", round(median_effect, 3)))
print(paste("95% CI: [", round(ci_lower, 3), ", ", round(ci_upper, 3), "]"))
print(paste("% in ROPE:", round(pct_in_rope, 1), "%"))

# The p-value and Bayesian analysis should now align
print("\nFREQUENTIST vs BAYESIAN ALIGNMENT:")
glmm_p <- glmm_summary$coefficients["pejorative_typeGOI", "Pr(>|z|)"]
print(paste("GLMM p-value:", round(glmm_p, 4)))
print(paste("Bayesian % in ROPE:", round(pct_in_rope, 1), "%"))

if(glmm_p > 0.05 && pct_in_rope > 75) {
  print("✓ Results are CONSISTENT: Both suggest no meaningful difference")
} else if(glmm_p < 0.05 && pct_in_rope < 25) {
  print("✓ Results are CONSISTENT: Both suggest meaningful difference")  
} else {
  print("⚠ Results still INCONSISTENT - further investigation needed")
}

# Create corrected publication plot
library(ggplot2)

density_data <- density(pejorative_samples_corrected, n = 1024)
density_df <- data.frame(x = density_data$x, y = density_data$y)

p_corrected <- ggplot(density_df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_rect(aes(xmin = rope_bounds[1], xmax = rope_bounds[2], 
                ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf),
            alpha = 0.3, fill = "lightgreen", color = NA) +
  geom_area(alpha = 0.7, fill = "steelblue", color = "darkblue", size = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", color = "black", size = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = rope_bounds, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "darkgreen", size = 0.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median_effect, linetype = "dotted", 
             color = "red", size = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = mean(rope_bounds), y = max(density_df$y) * 0.9, 
           label = "ROPE\n(Practical\nEquivalence)", 
           hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, size = 3.5, color = "darkgreen",
           fontface = "bold") +
  labs(
    title = "Bayesian Equivalence Test: Pejorative Type Effect",
    subtitle = paste0("Probability of practical equivalence: ", 
                     round(pct_in_rope, 1), "% | p-value: ", 
                     round(glmm_p, 3)),
    x = "Log-odds Difference (GOI vs Baseline)",
    y = "Posterior Density",
    caption = paste0("ROPE: ±", abs(rope_bounds[1]), " log-odds. ",
                    "95% CI: [", round(ci_lower, 2), ", ", 
                    round(ci_upper, 2), "]")
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "darkblue"),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray40"),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 11)
  )

print(p_corrected)

# Final interpretation
if(pct_in_rope > 90 && glmm_p > 0.10) {
  print("\nCONCLUSION: Strong evidence that pejorative types behave equivalently")
  print("Both Bayesian and frequentist approaches agree: no meaningful difference")
} else if(pct_in_rope > 75 && glmm_p > 0.05) {
  print("\nCONCLUSION: Moderate evidence that pejorative types behave equivalently") 
  print("Both approaches suggest no meaningful difference")
} else {
  print("\nCONCLUSION: Evidence is inconclusive or suggests meaningful difference")
}
```
